questions.prompt: When does PMax win vs lose?

choices.text: Wins: e-commerce with strong product feed, multi-surface customer journey, strong first-party audience signals, adequate creative throughput (5+ headlines, 4+ descriptions, multi-ratio images, 1+ video), $10k+/month spend with mature pixel signal. Loses: thin conversion volume (<30 conv/30 days), tiny creative library, B2B narrow audiences, need for granular reporting, strict compliance with placement requirements. Bad creative + thin audience + sparse feed = bad PMax regardless of automation.

questions.explanation: PMax fits e-commerce with feed, audience, creative volume. Loses on thin signal or B2B narrow.

questions.prompt: E-commerce account ROAS looks great on PMax but new-customer rate is flat. Likely cause and fix?

choices.text: PMax is repainting existing customers — claiming credit for purchases that would have happened anyway. Enable 'Customer Acquisition' mode: either 'Bid higher for new customers only' (still serves existing but de-prioritizes them) or 'Bid for new customers only' (excludes existing). Upload your full customer list as the existing-customer signal. ROAS may dip short-term (less inflation from existing buyers) but new-customer rate should climb. This is one of PMax's most underused settings.

questions.explanation: Enable Customer Acquisition mode with the existing-customer list uploaded.
